frameworks.name: deep neural network models of brain
frameworks.one_line: DNNs as learned-from-experience theories of perception, cognition, and action

frameworks.name: neural coding
frameworks.one_line: classical program of characterising neural codes and computations

core_question: How can neuroscientists use deep networks to model and understand biological brains in the age of deep learning?
key_assumptions: computations in deep networks are learned from experience, not endowed by the researcher
comparing artificial and biological systems on behaviour, learning, and representation is informative for theory
key_findings: machine learning advances open new ways of thinking about neural computation
DNNs may offer theories of perception, cognition, and action for biological brains
recent ML advances raise new research questions for systems neuroscience
interpretations: a DNN-based perspective could radically reshape how neuroscientists understand neural systems
the paper offers a roadmap for systems neuroscience in the age of deep learning
limitations_noted: conceptual and methodological challenges remain in comparing artificial and biological systems
